reproducible_artifact: An incident-commander runbook step + post-incident-review checklist item
embodiment_sketch: Runbook step (IC handbook, section: 'when the senior owner freezes'): 'If 5+ minutes pass with no runbook step executed AND the senior owner of the system is posting apology / self-blame messages in the channel, the IC interrupts and directs the next runbook step explicitly. Console-mode is reserved for after the page is closed.' Companion PRR checklist line: 'Did the senior owner freeze during the incident? Did the IC name the freeze and call the next runbook step? (yes/no/N-A)'
verdict_reason: This is a clean PASS. The verse's claim is encoded in (a) a literal runbook step a team can adopt this week, (b) a literal PRR checklist line, and (c) an IC training rubric. Tags include incident-response, on-call-doctrine — both structural, not state-of-mind-only. A senior engineer could add this to their team's actual practice on Monday.
